user_query:
I'm a parent volunteer helping organize our parish's religious education lessons. Please build a consolidated, validated index of the lesson Markdown files and write it to output/lesson_index.json, and also produce a validation report at output/validation_report.csv.

Inputs are all local in this workspace:
- input/lessons/*.md (Markdown with YAML front matter)
- input/config/allowed_taxonomy.py (Python file with allowed tags and age groups)
- input/config/theme_mapping.yaml (YAML mapping of keywords to liturgical themes, in priority order)
- input/config/scripture_book_to_testament.json (JSON mapping of scripture book names to testament)

Requirements:
1) Read every .md file under input/lessons and parse the YAML front matter. Required front-matter fields per lesson are: title (string), date (YYYY-MM-DD string), age_group (string), scripture (string), keywords (list of strings), tags (list of strings). If a required field is missing, record an issue and set valid=false for that lesson.
2) Determine scripture_book as the leading word token in the scripture field (substring up to the first space). Use input/config/scripture_book_to_testament.json to derive testament for that book. If the book is not present in the JSON mapping, set testament="Unknown" and record an issue.
3) Determine liturgical_theme by matching lesson keywords to input/config/theme_mapping.yaml:
   - Case-insensitive exact match of any keyword to the keyword entries in theme_mapping.yaml.
   - If multiple match, choose the first match according to the order defined in theme_mapping.yaml.
   - If none match, set liturgical_theme="Unmapped" (no issue needed for unmapped).
4) Validate taxonomy using input/config/allowed_taxonomy.py. Do not hardcode these values—import or read this file to obtain ALLOWED_TAGS and AGE_GROUPS.
   - age_group must be one of AGE_GROUPS; otherwise record an issue.
   - Every tag must be in ALLOWED_TAGS; record an issue for each unknown tag.
5) For each lesson, compute valid=true only if there are no recorded issues for that file; otherwise valid=false.

Outputs:
A) output/lesson_index.json: an array of objects—one per lesson—with the following fields for each lesson:
   - source_file (string, relative path like input/lessons/lesson_YYYY-MM-DD.md)
   - title (string)
   - date (string)
   - age_group (string)
   - scripture (string)
   - scripture_book (string)
   - testament (string)
   - keywords (array of strings)
   - tags (array of strings)
   - liturgical_theme (string)
   - valid (boolean)

B) output/validation_report.csv: a CSV with header and one row per issue detected, with columns:
   - source_file
   - issue_type (one of: missing_field, invalid_age_group, unknown_tag, unknown_scripture_book)
   - detail (brief description, e.g., which field is missing or which tag/book was unrecognized)

Notes:
- Do not modify any input files. Use only the local files provided. Do not use the Internet.
- Make sure the taxonomy (tags and age groups) comes from inspecting input/config/allowed_taxonomy.py, and the theme and testament values are derived from the provided YAML/JSON configs so someone else can re-run this and get the same results. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
